And those guys had a fair amount of spending authority, which surprised me when I found out about it. I mean, they could only go so far, but they had HRD budgets and they had equipment budgets. Well, we were selling them a replacement product for the tarp on the back of the truck that keeps the gravel or the coal or whatever the hell it is coming out and cracking a windshield. That doesn't surprise me. What surprises me, you're selling them information marketing products, right? Info products to middle management people. And normally middle management people do not have authority. I'll give you another analogy. American back school, we were selling ergonomic education stuff. How to teach the guys on your crew to lift properly so they don't hurt their backs, that kind of stuff. And we were selling DVDs and videos and audios and stuff. And again, that factory supervisor guy, a lot of them had the ability to spend a certain amount of money on their own and or they had to go just one level. I'll read the whole thing, but there was one insight that was, you know, I hadn't really thought of before. John Agent was married with two to three children. He values family, above all, and wishes he could spend more time with them. He has relatively conservative political values. He's active in his local community and highly values his personal and professional reputation. He believes that hard work leads to success, but often regrets that he works so hard. He would describe himself as honest, industrious, and friendly. He greatly enjoys public recognition for a job well done. Secret fear is not making enough money or making as much as he wants. So in terms of politics and religion, you are not really going to touch on those unless you're pretty sure that from the vast majority of your subscribers really helps you. Yeah, look, see I think the advice of never speak about politics, sex, and religion, I always thought it was stupid because pretty much what else are you going to talk about?
